publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-03-18 23:59:12 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Norman D. Daly (August 9, 1911 - April 2, 2008), was an American artist who created the fictional ancient Civilization of Llhuros along with hundreds of its artifacts. His work on The Civilization of Llhuros starting in the mid 1960s makes him the pioneering practitioner of an art genre now known as fictive archaeology. In 1942, Daly joined the Department of Art at Cornell University in Ithaca, New York, where he taught drawing, painting, materials and methods, and elements of design. He became Professor of Art in 1958 and retired in 1976, but continued to teach as an emeritus professor until 1999.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Documents, photographs, ephemera, and recordings relating to the development of the Llhuros Civilization, as well as material from Daly's time at Cornell.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Norman Daly papers, #15-2-4489. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Student material in Box 6 is restricted until 2075.

controlaccess.subject: Archaeology
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University.College of Architecture, Art, and Planning.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Llhuros II refers to Daly's continued creation of new objects that eventually related to the play "Stiltwalkers."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes unused notes and drafts for catalogs and publications as well as samples of Llhuroscian writing.

did.unittitle: Enumeration of Llhuroscian objects by period
Original sketch for "The Map of Llhuros"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a sketch of Llhuroiscian cuneiform.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pre-Llhuroscian constructions which were titled and later assimilated by the Civilization of Llhuros collection

did.unittitle: History of Llhuros slide show list
List of slides submitted for an unknown grant application

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains documents and correspondence relating to Llhuros and Cologne portfolio, including drawings and plans.

did.unittitle: College Art Association correspondence and presentation
"Adventures in Related Sciences" script for radio show

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials likely relating to Daly's play "Stiltwalkers." Includes brief description of the play from 1997. Also includes information about the Virgin Self-Portraits/Colored Ink Series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes article about source of materials for objects COL 82 "Univell in Ulbro" and COL 101 "Ossified Purple Seahorse"

did.unittitle: Object photographs, likely for Llhuros II
Slides of Llhuroscian objects, exhibitions, and Llhuros II mixed media

did.unittitle: Herbert F. Johnson Museum of Art exhibition photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from museum director Frank Robinson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The cover design was by Daly, the photograph by Andrea Strongwater. This was the first public exposure to a Llhuroscian object and poetry, Object #7 - Fornicating Gods: early archaic-metal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten page by Daly, and Daly's edits on a photocopy version of the catalog text

did.unittitle: General articles about the Civilization of Llhuros

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an inventory of items in folder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pre-publication documents for an article on the Civilization of Llhuros, as well as a copy of the final article and related correspondence from Beauvais Lyons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains article about Naglee Fine Arts.

did.unittitle: Sheet music for the Virgin's Chorus and Darkdow Children's Chorus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence about recording equipment, and an original score copied from Daly's score by Elizabeth (Bets) Fisher.

did.unittitle: Unedited sounds: virgins, water, storm, etc.
Spoken description of further work for grant proposal
Spoken description of further work for grant proposal, description of temple virgins, Itnasov's narrative, Enota's poem
Excerpts of soundtrack, slide show narration
Interview with Nils Schilaneder, used as part of background for Llhuros
Interview with Nils Schilaneder, used as part of background for Llhuros
Llhuros slide show, Richard Korf reading
Lost Civilization of Llhuros, Johnson Museum of Art
Lost Civilization of Llhuros, Johnson Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disk 01, converted from VHS copy. Document's Daly's one-man exhibition of "The Civilization of Llhuros and Other Works" at the Johnson Museum in 2004.

did.unittitle: Lost Civilization of Llhuros, Johnson Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recording consists of 3 unrealted sections: 1. 'Government Appointment' Dream (0:00-6:14) - Daly relates dream involving his government appointment where he commanded people to prove to themselves who they think they are, in order to fulfill their roles over others. 2. The Power of the Vertical (6:15-15:26) - Daly explains the power of the vertical in relation to the horizontal in architecture, flying buttresses, fenestration,. stained glass windows, rose window, Notre Dame cathedral 3. Horse People of Llhuros (15:27-18:33) - While not identifying things in so many words, these brief comments concern the Horse People of Llhuros who were forbidden to eat the flesh of a type of fish (purple seahorse) but did so anyway. See Civilization of Llhuros 1972 catalogue narrative for lengthy presentation on the Horse People.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A partial recording of Daly reading the script titles, directions, etc. then doing the full dialogue of part of Scene 1. Characters are Malcolm and daughter Lou sparring over aspects of Llhuroscian scholarship. Malcolm reads some Llhroscian poetry.

did.unittitle: David Borden Materials for Daly's C.O.L. Soundtracks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Higher-fidelity versions of other sound sources. Using the Moog synthesizer, Borden created material for Daly to manipulate for his soundtrack.

did.unittitle: Civilization of Llhuros (C.O.L.) Slide Show Narration - Copy 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Korf (Cornell mycology professor, actor and frequent Daly collaborator) recites selections from the 1972 COL catalog and narrative Llhuroscian poetry. Slides of Llhuroscian objects are mentioned. First few lines of the text are missing but can be found in copy 2.

did.unittitle: Civilization of Llhuros (C.O.L.) Slide Show Narration - Copy 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of Slide Show Narration identical except for part of the first sentence which is missing on copy 1.

did.unittitle: Llhuroscian Poetry Rehearsal with Richard Korf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to Slide Show Narration tapes. Here, Richard Korf rehearses poems and recites some selections of narrative and poems from the 1972 Civilization of Llhuros catalog, while Norman Daly plays tapes of music fragments from the COL soundscape in the background. Includes: "Shisun make me one...", "If you want me...", "Offer me a shroud...", "Evening song...", "In a sacred fog..."

did.unittitle: Sound Effects for Use in COL Exhibition Soundtracks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twelve natural sounds provided at Daly's request by the Cornell Lab or Orinthology (mid to late 1960s).

did.unittitle: Stilt-Walkers reading at Cornell (2 VHS tapes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviews, catalogs, lists of paintings sold, and letter from Bertha Schaefer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains reviews, catalogs, and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Willard Straight Hall gallery, Mortimer Brandt Gallery, and various others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains documents from the New York State Fair (1950), Cortland County Fair (1953), Corcoran Gallery (1959), Utica Arts Festival, Munson-Williams-Proctor Institute (1959), and St. Lawrence University (1955).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Franklin Hall gallery (1962), Draper Hall gallery at SUNY Albany (1964), and correspondence concerning ads to be taken out.

scopecontent.p: Includes proposal for the donation of Daly's mural design to Cornell Universtiy for installation.

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113902898]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains cards on Durand-Ruel Exhibition (1949), Bertha Schaefer (1950), and Mortimer Brandt (1945).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a spread of the living room of Robert L. Myers, architect and art collector, that features an unidentified painting by Daly on the wall at the far right. Myers was a student of Daly while he was studying architecture at Cornell in the early 1950s.

did.unittitle: Tobias and the Angel, Asheville Art Museum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents about Asheville Art Museum's acquisition of "Tobias and the Angel."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a 1972 prelim exam given to student sin Anthro 200 by Robert Ascher, Llhuros figured prominently in the course material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Daly's correspondence on his concerns, related newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly Llhuros related.

did.unittitle: Student "fan mail" related to Daly's retirement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes quote from Thomas Leavitt about conceptual art.

did.unittitle: Cornell University Summer Program in Art poster
Cornell University Figure and Landscape Summer Program in Art
Norman Daly's introduction to Tom Leavitt recording

container.altrender: Oversize Letter Upright Box, (10.25 x 7 x 12.25)
container.label: Mixed Materials [31924113902898]

did.unittitle: Class Lectures on Form and Content

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Pictorial Analysis" Course at Cornell Art Department. Recording begins mid-sentence but settles in quickly into showing paintings to the class for analysis. Defines the difference between form and content (content is not the subject matter, but the meaning the artist intends).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lecture for Daly's Cornell Art Department Course, "Pictorial Analysis." Energetic, wide-ranging discussion in which Daily begins by asking "What is Art?"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Class lecture from Cornell Art Department Course.

did.unittitle: Art vs. Religion/Introduction for Michael Goldfarb Visit to Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three unrelated sections: 1. Art vs. Religion, 2. Anecdote about an "un-named Dean," 3. Daly's introduction for journalist Michael Goldfarb who would be speaking at Cornell.

did.unittitle: Norman Daly and Kenneth Evett: Professors Analyze a Painting by Vermeer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Norman Daly and Kenneth Evett (close friends and professors in the Cornell Art Department) give a pictoral analysis of Vermeer's "Girl Reading a Letter at an Open Widow." It appears they were planning to do a presentation because they discussed diagrams. No diagrams related to this have been found.

did.unittitle: Art notes and drawings by Professor Joseph Mellor Hanson
San Cristobal Petroglyphs, Agnes C. Sims. Southwest Editions, Santa Fe, exhibition catalog

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Catalogue, "Very relevant to the Southwestern influences in ND's paintings of the 1940s."

did.unittitle: "The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist
"The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist
"The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist
"The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist
"The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist
"The Civilization of Llhuros" project film by Kells Elmquist, mini-DV recording
Series VI. Daly Family and Associates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vital records, correspondence, photography, exhibit materials, catalogs, and other ephemera related to Daly's personal life and his friendships and associates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview of Daly by Bill Monroe, Daly's health aid from 2004 until Daly's death in 2008. Interview recording consists of 2 sections: 1. 'Red-Bellied Snakes' Deam (0:00-5:56) and 2. Early Life (5:57-1:02:11).

did.unittitle: Subseries VIc. Materials by name or subject

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various individuals, materials seperated by name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Various individuals, materials seperated by name.
